name: mixed_ruby_bouten

meta.note: [provenance:hand-derived §6.1+§6.2 + AOZORA-ANNOTATION 2026-06-17] The only 青梅 preceding the bracket is the ruby base, so the forward bouten target resolves to it: a non-adjacent target that is itself a ruby base, which per ADR 0003 §6.2 stays Referenced (bouten-over-ruby is not representable, so it cannot fold into a text-only forward leaf). HTML therefore renders 青梅 once — inside the ruby base — and drops the emphasis markup; re-rendering the target would double 青梅 (the duplication bug fixed in reference impl #228). The bouten node still exists (the bracket round-trips), so serialize re-emits the bracket form unchanged with the literal left upstream. Offsets from the §3 byte model. Serialize canonicalises right-side ruby to the BARE form (the explicit ｜ is dropped) per ADR 0003 §6.1 / ADR 0002: ｜ is emitted only when a bare 《reading》 would re-parse to a different base; here every base is an all-kanji run whose preceding char is non-base (line start / 　 / の), so the bare form re-parses identically.

source: ｜青梅《おうめ》には［＃「青梅」に傍点］という街道がある。

expected.html: 青梅(おうめ)にはという街道がある。
expected.serialize: 青梅《おうめ》には［＃「青梅」に傍点］という街道がある。
